common.appDescription: A collection of useful tools for everyone
common.noFavoritesDescription: Click the star icon on any tool to add it to favorites

cookieConsent.title: 🍪 We Use Cookies
cookieConsent.description: We use cookies and similar technologies to improve your experience, show personalized ads, and analyze traffic. By clicking 'Accept', you consent to our use of cookies.

about.title: About Me
about.subtitle: Uğurcan Güden
about.description: I'm Uğurcan Güden, a software developer focusing on .NET/microservices and React/Next.js. I work on scalability, security, and performance across projects like QQRCHEF and Free Dev Tools. I also maintain guden-core (NPM) and Guden.Lib* (NuGet) to standardize common needs.

frontend.title: Frontend Development
frontend.description: Building dynamic, responsive and user-friendly interfaces with React, Next.js and modern JavaScript frameworks. Creating modern UI/UX solutions with Ant Design and Tailwind CSS.

backend.title: Backend Development
backend.description: Designing secure, scalable and high-performance backend systems with .NET Core, microservice architectures, EF Core, Redis and PostgreSQL/MSSQL. Developing security-focused solutions with JWT and role-based auth.

devops.title: DevOps & Deployment
devops.description: Setting up CI/CD pipelines with Docker container technology, custom image registry, Nginx reverse proxy, GitHub Actions and Azure DevOps. Creating scalable and secure deployment processes.

mobile.title: Open Source
mobile.description: Providing reusable, easy-to-maintain and standard solutions with guden-core (NPM) and Guden.Lib* (NuGet) packages. I value sharing with the community and continuous learning.

tools.title: QQRCHEF & Free Dev Tools
tools.description: Among the projects I'm actively developing are QQRCHEF (.NET Core + Next.js) and Free Dev Tools (browser-based free tools for developers).

principle1.title: Honesty
principle1.description: Honesty and transparency in work and relationships are always priorities for me.

principle2.title: Discipline
principle2.description: I value the discipline of continuous learning, regular work and quality-focused development.

principle3.title: Responsibility
principle3.description: I take ownership of tasks and aim to contribute to team success.

principle4.title: Team Harmony
principle4.description: I'm a collaborative and learning-oriented team member. I believe in doing better together.

contact.title: Contact
contact.description: Reach out for ideas, suggestions and collaborations!
contact.message: Let's share ideas and create great things together! 🚀

howToUse: Paste your raw JSON code into the editor.
Click 'Format' to beautify or 'Minify' to compress.
The 'Tree' view allows you to explore complex objects easily.
features: Syntax highlighting and error detection.
Collapsible tree view for nested data.
One-click 'Escape' for stringified JSON.
Secure client-side validation.
seo.privacy: All JSON formatting and validation happens in your browser. Your data is never sent to our servers, ensuring your sensitive API responses and configuration files stay private.

faqs.question: How do I format JSON in one click?
faqs.answer: Simply paste your JSON code into the editor and click the 'Format' button. The tool will instantly beautify your JSON with proper indentation.

faqs.question: Can I validate JSON syntax errors?
faqs.answer: Yes! The formatter automatically highlights syntax errors as you type, showing you exactly which line contains the invalid JSON.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Format and Minify?
faqs.answer: Format (or Beautify) adds indentation and line breaks to make JSON readable. Minify removes all whitespace to create the smallest possible JSON string, ideal for production APIs.

faqs.question: Can I handle very large JSON files?
faqs.answer: Yes, the editor supports large JSON files efficiently. For files larger than a few MB, we recommend using the tree view to navigate the structure without needing to scroll through raw text.

xmlFormatter.title: XML Formatter

xmlFormatter.aboutDescription: XML (eXtensible Markup Language) is a markup language that defines rules for encoding documents in a format that is both human-readable and machine-readable.

seo.privacy: Your generator settings and output remain private in your browser session. No data is stored.

faqs.question: What is Lorem Ipsum?
faqs.answer: Lorem Ipsum is a placeholder text commonly used in the graphic, print, and publishing industries for previewing layouts and visual mockups.

faqs.question: Does this generator support different languages?
faqs.answer: While it generates traditional Latin-based text, the interface is available in multiple languages.

caseConverter.description: Convert text between different case styles

howToUse: Type or paste your text into the input area.
Click on the conversion buttons or watch real-time updates.
Copy specific converted text or download all formats.
features: Sentence case, lower case, UPPER CASE transformations.
Capitalized Case and Title Case support.
Alternating cAsE and iNVERSE cASE for unique styling.
One-click copying and TXT file download.
seo.privacy: Your text is processed locally in your browser. No data is sent to our servers, keeping your content 100% private.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Title Case and Capitalized Case?
faqs.answer: Title Case typically follows specific capitalization rules for titles (like 'The Catcher in the Rye'), while Capitalized Case usually capitalizes every single word regardless of its function.

faqs.question: Does it support non-English characters?
faqs.answer: Yes, our Case Converter works with most UTF-8 characters, including accented letters and various alphabets.

info.title: About Case Converter
info.description: Convert text between different case styles instantly.

howToUse: Paste your messy HTML code into the input field.
Click 'Format' to beautify your code with proper indentation.
Use 'Minify' to compress HTML for production use.
Copy the result to your clipboard or download it.
features: Auto-indentation for better readability.
Instant minification to save bytes.
Handles nested tags and attributes correctly.
Works entirely in your browser for fast processing.
seo.privacy: Your HTML code is processed entirely on your machine. We never store or transmit your code to any server.

faqs.question: Can it fix broken HTML tags?
faqs.answer: The formatter tries its best to structure the code, but it doesn't automatically fix missing closing tags or syntax errors. It focuses on pretty-printing valid HTML.

faqs.question: Is there a limit to the size of HTML I can format?
faqs.answer: Since it runs in your browser, it can handle very large files (up to several MBs), depending on your computer's memory.

info.title: About HTML Formatter
info.description: Format, beautify, and minify HTML code with proper indentation.

howToUse: Paste your CSS code into the input field.
Click 'Minify' to compress or 'Beautify' to format.
Copy the result or check the size savings statistics.
features: Removes comments and unnecessary whitespace.
Compresses selectors and properties into a single line.
Beautify mode for readable code formatting.
Real-time file size savings calculation.
seo.privacy: Your CSS code remains in your browser. We don't track or store any code you process on our platform.

faqs.question: Will minifying my CSS break the site?
faqs.answer: Minification only removes unnecessary characters like spaces and comments. It does not change the logic of your CSS, so it should not break your site.

faqs.question: What is the best way to use minified CSS?
faqs.answer: Use minified CSS for your production website to reduce page load times and improve performance.

info.title: About CSS Minifier
info.description: Minify CSS to reduce file size and improve loading speed.

seo.privacy: All SQL formatting is done locally. Your database structure and query logic are never sent to a server.

faqs.question: Which SQL dialects are supported?
faqs.answer: It supports standard ANSI SQL, which covers the majority of keywords used in MySQL, PostgreSQL, and SQL Server.

faqs.question: Does it format complex nested queries?
faqs.answer: Yes, the formatter handles subqueries and complex joins by applying consistent indentation levels.

wordCounter.title: Word & Character Counter
wordCounter.description: Count words, characters, sentences, and paragraphs

howToUse: Paste or type your text into the input area.
Check the statistics box for real-time updates on word, character, and line counts.
Use the 'Clear' button to start over with new text.
features: Real-time word and character counting.
Line and paragraph count detection.
Reading time and speaking time estimation.
100% Client-side processing for ultimate privacy.
seo.privacy: Our Word Counter tool processes all text locally within your browser. We never upload your content to any server, ensuring your sensitive documents stay private and secure.

faqs.question: How accurate is the word counter?
faqs.answer: Our word counter splits text by whitespace and punctuation to give an accurate count. It handles multiple spaces, newlines, and hyphenated words correctly.

faqs.question: Does the word counter work offline?
faqs.answer: Yes! All processing happens entirely in your browser. Once the page loads, you can use the word counter without an internet connection.

faqs.question: Can I count characters without spaces?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. The tool displays both total characters (including spaces) and characters excluding spaces in the statistics panel.

faqs.question: Is my text saved anywhere?
faqs.answer: No. Your text is never sent to any server. Everything is processed client-side, so your content remains completely private.

wordCounter.inputPlaceholder: Enter or paste your text here...
wordCounter.keywordDensityDescription: Top keywords excluding common stop words.

info.title: About Word Counter
info.description: Count words, characters, sentences, and paragraphs. Also estimates reading time.

howToUse: Enter your list (one item per line) into the input area.
Select a sorting method: A-Z, Z-A, Numeric, or Random.
Use 'Reverse' to flip the current order.
Click 'Remove Duplicates' to clean up your list instantly.
features: Multiple sorting algorithms (Lexicographic, Numeric).
Random shuffle for generating unique sequences.
Integrated duplicate removal logic.
Ultra-fast processing for long lists.
seo.privacy: Your lists stay in your browser. We don't track, store, or see the data you sort.

faqs.question: Can I sort numbers correctly?
faqs.answer: Yes, use the 'Numeric' sort button to ensure numbers like 10 appear after 2, instead of alphabetically.

faqs.question: How do I reverse a sorted list?
faqs.answer: Simply click the 'Reverse' button after sorting to invert the order of all lines.

info.title: About Line Sorter
info.description: Sort text lines alphabetically, numerically, or randomly. Also supports reversing and removing duplicates.

howToUse: Paste your raw data into the input box.
Toggle 'Case Sensitive' if you want 'Apple' and 'apple' treated as different.
Choose whether to keep the 'First Occurred' or 'Last Occurred' line.
View the summary of lines removed and copy the unique result.
features: Smart case sensitivity toggle.
Occurrence management (Keep First vs Last).
Real-time statistics (Original vs Unique vs Removed).
One-click result copying.
seo.privacy: All duplicate detection happens locally in your browser. Your sensitive lists are never uploaded.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Keep First and Keep Last?
faqs.answer: 'Keep First' retains the first time a duplicate appears and deletes subsequent ones. 'Keep Last' does the opposite, keeping the newest entry.

faqs.question: Can this handle white spaces?
faqs.answer: The tool treats exact matches of lines. If lines have different leading or trailing spaces, they will be considered unique unless you trim them first.

info.title: About Duplicate Remover
info.description: Remove duplicate lines from text while preserving order. Choose to keep first or last occurrence.

seo.howToUseTitle: How to Use HTML Entity Encoder?
howToUse: Paste text with special characters to 'Encode' them for HTML.
Paste encoded entities (like &lt;) to 'Decode' them back to characters.
Use the Entity Reference table for quickly finding common codes.
Copy the resulting text for use in your web projects.
features: Bidirectional processing (Encode & Decode).
Comprehensive reference table for common entities.
Supports special characters, symbols, and currency signs.
Instant processing without server roundtrips.
seo.privacy: Encoding and decoding happen entirely within your local browser environment. No text is sent to our servers.

faqs.question: Why do I need to encode HTML entities?
faqs.answer: Special characters like < or > have meaning in HTML. Encoding them ensures they are displayed as text rather than being interpreted as tags.

faqs.question: Does it support all characters?
faqs.answer: It supports standard HTML named entities and can decode any valid HTML entity back to its original character.

info.title: About HTML Entity Encoder
info.description: Convert special characters to HTML entities and vice versa. Useful for displaying special characters in HTML.

Sample card numbers included for testing.
Completely private client-side validation.
seo.privacy: Credit card numbers are processed only on your computer. We do not store, log, or transmit any card information.

faqs.question: Does this validator check the bank balance?
faqs.answer: No. This tool only checks the mathematical validity of the card number using the Luhn algorithm. It does not contact any financial institution or check balances.

faqs.question: Is it safe to enter my card number here?
faqs.answer: Yes, because the validation happens entirely in your browser using JavaScript. Your card number is never sent to our servers.

pdfToWord.title: PDF to Word
pdfToWord.description: Convert PDF files to editable Word documents

seo.howToUseTitle: How to Convert PDF to Word
howToUse: Upload your PDF file using the drag-and-drop area or file selector.
Click the 'Convert' button to start the transformation process.
Download your editable .docx file once the conversion is complete.
seo.featuresTitle: Why Use Our PDF to Word Converter?
features: High-quality conversion preserving the original layout.
Fast processing using advanced OCR technology.
Support for large PDF files with secure handling.
No registration or email required for free use.
seo.privacy: We prioritize your security. Your files are processed on our secure servers and are automatically deleted after the conversion is complete. We do not store or share your documents.

faqs.question: What is the maximum PDF file size I can convert?
faqs.answer: You can upload PDF files up to 50MB. For larger files, we recommend splitting the PDF before converting.

faqs.question: Will the formatting be preserved after conversion?
faqs.answer: Our converter uses advanced technology to preserve text layout, fonts, and tables as accurately as possible. Complex graphics may have slight variations.

faqs.question: How long does it take to convert a PDF?
faqs.answer: Most conversions complete within a few seconds to a minute, depending on the file size and complexity.

faqs.question: Are my files stored on the server?
faqs.answer: No. Your uploaded files are automatically and permanently deleted from our servers immediately after processing is complete.

base64.title: Base64 Encoder/Decoder
base64.description: Encode and decode Base64 strings and files

howToUse: Enter your plain text to encode or Base64 string to decode.
Use the 'Encode' button for text-to-binary or 'Decode' for binary-to-text.
Copy the result instantly using the copy button.
features: Bidirectional encoding and decoding.
Support for special characters and emojis.
Real-time processing with instant results.
Secure and private browser-based execution.
seo.privacy: Our Base64 tool operates entirely within your browser's local environment. No data is transmitted over the internet, making it safe for encoding sensitive configuration values.

faqs.question: What is Base64 encoding used for?
faqs.answer: Base64 is widely used to encode binary data for safe transmission in text-based systems, such as embedding images in HTML/CSS, encoding file attachments in emails, and passing data in URL query parameters.

faqs.question: Can I decode any Base64 string?
faqs.answer: Yes. Paste any valid Base64-encoded string into the input field and switch to 'Decode' mode to reveal the original content. The tool supports standard and URL-safe Base64 variants.

faqs.question: Can I encode files to Base64?
faqs.answer: Yes! You can upload a file using the file picker and the tool will encode it to Base64, which you can then copy and embed directly in your code.

faqs.question: Is Base64 the same as encryption?
faqs.answer: No. Base64 is an encoding scheme, not encryption. It does not protect your data — anyone who has the Base64 string can easily decode it. For security, use proper encryption methods.

urlEncoder.title: URL Encoder/Decoder
urlEncoder.description: Encode and decode URLs and query parameters

howToUse: Select the number of UUIDs you want to generate using the slider.
Choose your formatting options like uppercase and hyphens.
Click 'Generate' to create new IDs and copy them to your clipboard.
features: RFC 4122 compliant v4 identifiers.
Bulk generation up to 1000 IDs at once.
One-click validation of existing UUIDs.
Instant copying for developer workflows.
seo.privacy: All UUIDs are generated using the cryptographically secure random number generator in your browser. No data is sent to any server during the generation process.

faqs.question: What is a UUID v4?
faqs.answer: UUID v4 (Universally Unique Identifier version 4) is a 128-bit identifier generated using random numbers. It follows the format xxxxxxxx-xxxx-4xxx-yxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx and is guaranteed to be practically unique.

faqs.question: How many UUIDs can I generate at once?
faqs.answer: You can generate up to 1000 UUIDs in a single batch using the slider or number input in the generator settings.

faqs.question: Are the generated UUIDs truly unique?
faqs.answer: UUID v4 uses cryptographically secure random numbers, making the probability of a collision astronomically low — statistically, you would need to generate quintillions of UUIDs before encountering a duplicate.

faqs.question: Can I copy all UUIDs at once?
faqs.answer: Yes! After generating multiple UUIDs, click the 'Copy All' button to copy all of them to your clipboard as a newline-separated list.

colorConverter.title: Color Converter
colorConverter.description: Convert colors between HEX, RGB, HSL and other formats

howToUse: Select a color using the picker or enter a value in HEX, RGB, or HSL.
Instantly see the converted values in various formats.
Explore shades, complementary colors, and harmonies.
Copy any color value with a single click.
features: Support for HEX, RGB, HSL, HSV, and CMYK.
Visual color picker and quick palette.
Generates 10% to 90% shades automatically.
Analogous, triadic, and complementary harmony suggestions.
seo.privacy: Color selections are processed locally. No personal data or preferences are tracked on our servers.

faqs.question: What is CMYK used for?
faqs.answer: CMYK is primarily used for color printing. Unlike digital screens (RGB), printers use Cyan, Magenta, Yellow, and Black inks to create colors.

faqs.question: How do I choose a harmonious color scheme?
faqs.answer: Use our harmony suggestions! Complementary colors create high contrast, while analogous colors provide a more harmonious and soothing look.

colorConverter.aboutDescription: Different color formats are used for different purposes. HEX for web, RGB for digital displays, CMYK for printing.
colorConverter.hexDescription: Most common format used on the web
colorConverter.rgbDescription: For digital screens and graphic design
colorConverter.hslDescription: Intuitive for color selection and manipulation

qrGenerator.title: QR Code Generator
qrGenerator.description: Generate QR codes for text and URLs

seo.howToUseTitle: How to Create a QR Code
howToUse: Enter the text or URL you want to encode into the input box.
Customize the size, colors, and error correction level in settings.
Download your finished QR code as a high-quality PNG or SVG file.
features: Adjustable size and margin settings.
Custom foreground and background colors.
Multiple error correction levels (L, M, Q, H).
Export as vector SVG or pixel PNG.
seo.privacy: Our QR generator works locally in your browser. The data you encode is never transmitted to our servers, making it safe for private information.

faqs.question: What types of content can a QR code store?
faqs.answer: QR codes can store URLs, plain text, email addresses, SMS messages, Wi-Fi credentials, vCard contact information, and more. Our generator accepts any text or URL.

faqs.question: Can I customize the colors of my QR code?
faqs.answer: Yes! You can choose custom foreground and background colors for your QR code using the color pickers in the settings panel. Ensure sufficient contrast for reliable scanning.

faqs.question: What error correction level should I choose?
faqs.answer: For most uses, 'Medium' (15%) is a good balance. Use 'High' (30%) if your QR code will be printed on surfaces that may get dirty or damaged, as it allows more of the code to be obscured while still being scannable.

faqs.question: Can I download the QR code as a vector file?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can download your QR code as an SVG (vector) file for lossless scaling at any size, in addition to PNG for raster graphics.

qrGenerator.inputPlaceholder: Enter text or URL for QR code...
qrGenerator.noQRCode: Enter text and click 'Generate QR Code' to create a QR code
qrGenerator.aboutDescription: QR (Quick Response) codes are two-dimensional barcodes used to store information and read it quickly.
qrGenerator.errorCorrectionDescription: Higher error correction level allows the QR code to be readable even when damaged or partially covered, but creates a more complex code.

passwordGenerator.title: Password Generator

howToUse: Choose between 'Password' (random characters) or 'Passphrase' (random words).
Adjust the length slider and toggle character sets (Numbers, Symbols, etc.).
For passphrases, select the number of words and a separator.
Instantly copy your strong new password or generate multiple at once.
features: Cryptographically secure randomness using Web Crypto API.
Rich customization: Exclude similar (i, l, 1) or ambiguous characters.
Detailed strength analysis with entropy bits and time-to-crack estimates.
Passphrase mode for memorable yet highly secure keys.
seo.privacy: Passwords are generated using your computer's entropy. They are never transmitted, logged, or visible to our team.

faqs.question: Which is better: Password or Passphrase?
faqs.answer: Passwords are better for machines and password managers. Passphrases are easier for humans to remember while maintaining extreme security through length.

faqs.question: What is entropy?
faqs.answer: Entropy is a measure of randomness. The higher the entropy (in bits), the more difficult it is for a hacker to guess your password using brute force.

passwordGenerator.excludeSimilar: Exclude Similar Characters (i, l, 1, L, o, 0, O)
passwordGenerator.aboutDescription: Strong passwords should be long, include a mix of uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and symbols, and be unpredictable.
passwordGenerator.tip2: Mix uppercase, lowercase, numbers, and symbols
passwordGenerator.tip4: Use different passwords for each account
passwordGenerator.selectAtLeastOne: You must select at least one character type

hashGenerator.title: Hash Generator
hashGenerator.description: Generate cryptographic hashes (MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-512)

howToUse: Paste your text into the input field or upload a file.
Check the real-time hash results for MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, and SHA-512.
Use the comparison tool to verify if two hashes match.
Enable HMAC to generate keyed-hash message authentication codes.
features: Supports multiple algorithms: MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-512.
File hashing capability for integrity verification.
Built-in HMAC support for secure authentication.
Instant hash comparison and status indicators.
seo.privacy: All hashing calculations are performed locally in your browser. Your sensitive text and files never leave your computer.

faqs.question: Is it safe to hash sensitive passwords here?
faqs.answer: Yes, our tool runs 100% on the client side. We don't have a backend to receive your data, ensuring your secrets remain private.

faqs.question: Which algorithm is the most secure?
faqs.answer: SHA-256 and SHA-512 are currently considered industry standard for security. MD5 and SHA-1 should only be used for non-security related integrity checks.

hashGenerator.aboutDescription: Cryptographic hash functions convert data into fixed-size strings. They are one-way functions, meaning you cannot reverse them to get the original data.
hashGenerator.hmacDescription: HMAC combines a cryptographic hash function with a secret key to provide message authentication. It's used to verify both data integrity and authentication.

cronExpressionGenerator.title: Cron Expression Generator & Explainer
cronExpressionGenerator.description: Generate, validate and explain 5-field cron expressions in plain language.
cronExpressionGenerator.placeholder: Use 5 fields: minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week
cronExpressionGenerator.parseError: Could not parse this cron expression.

nextRuns.title: Next Execution Times
nextRuns.empty: Enter a valid cron expression to see upcoming runs.

examples.title: Popular Cron Examples
items: 0 0 1 * * -> Runs on the first day of each month

whatIsCron.title: What is a Cron Expression?
whatIsCron.body: A cron expression is a compact schedule format used to define when a job should run. It is commonly used in servers, automation tools, CI/CD pipelines and cloud schedulers.

starsMeaning.title: What do the cron stars mean? (Minute, Hour, Day...)
starsMeaning.body: In cron, * means any value for that field. In a 5-field expression, the fields are minute, hour, day of month, month and day of week. You can also use ranges, lists and step values like */5.

introduction.title: Introduction
introduction.content1: Welcome to Toolbox. We respect your privacy and are committed to protecting your personal data. This privacy policy explains how we handle your information when you use our website.
introduction.content2: Toolbox is a collection of free online tools. All processing happens locally in your browser. We use cookies only for advertising purposes (Google AdSense) and do not collect any personal identifiable information.

blog.title: Blog
blog.subtitle: Tools, Formats and Best Practices Guide

what-is-json.title: What is JSON? JavaScript Object Notation Guide
what-is-json.description: JSON (JavaScript Object Notation) format, history, use cases and examples. The cornerstone of data exchange in modern web applications.
what-is-json.content: JSON (JavaScript Object Notation) is a lightweight, human-readable, and platform-independent data format used for data exchange in modern web applications. Developed by Douglas Crockford in the early 2000s, it has become the de facto standard for web APIs today. JSON is based on JavaScript's object notation syntax and emerged as a simpler and more readable alternative to XML.

what-is-xml.title: What is XML? Extensible Markup Language Guide
what-is-xml.description: XML (Extensible Markup Language) format, history, use cases and best practices. The powerful standard for data transport and storage.
what-is-xml.content: XML (Extensible Markup Language) is a structured data format that is both human and machine-readable, standardized by W3C in 1998. Unlike HTML, it allows user-defined tags and is focused on data transport. XML was designed as a simplified version of SGML (Standard Generalized Markup Language).

what-is-base64.title: What is Base64? Encoding and Decoding Guide
what-is-base64.description: Base64 encoding system, how it works, use cases and examples. Essential for binary data transfer in text-based systems.
what-is-base64.content: Base64 is an encoding scheme that converts binary data into ASCII text format. It's widely used for transferring binary data over text-based protocols like email, JSON, and XML. Base64 uses 64 characters (A-Z, a-z, 0-9, +, /) to represent binary data.

what-is-uuid.title: What is UUID? Universally Unique Identifier Guide
what-is-uuid.description: UUID (Universally Unique Identifier) format, versions, generation methods and use cases. The standard for unique identification.
what-is-uuid.content: UUID (Universally Unique Identifier) is a 128-bit number used to uniquely identify information in computer systems. Also known as GUID (Globally Unique Identifier), UUIDs are designed to be unique across space and time without requiring a central authority.

what-is-hash.title: What is Hash? Cryptographic Hash Functions Guide
what-is-hash.description: Hash functions, MD5, SHA-256, use cases and security. Essential for data integrity and password security.
what-is-hash.content: A hash function is a mathematical algorithm that converts input data of any size into a fixed-size output (hash value or digest). Cryptographic hash functions like MD5, SHA-1, and SHA-256 are one-way functions used for data integrity, password storage, and digital signatures.

what-is-jwt.title: What is JWT? JSON Web Token Security Guide
what-is-jwt.description: JWT (JSON Web Token) structure, use cases and security. The standard for authentication in modern web applications.
what-is-jwt.content: JWT (JSON Web Token) is an open standard (RFC 7519) that provides a secure way to transmit information between parties. It's primarily used for authentication and information exchange. JWT consists of three parts: Header, Payload, and Signature.

what-is-regex.title: What is Regex? Comprehensive Regular Expressions Guide
what-is-regex.description: Regular Expression (Regex) definition, how to use, examples and best practices. Powerful tool for text processing and pattern matching.
what-is-regex.content: Regular Expression (Regex) is a powerful tool for searching, matching, and replacing specific patterns in text. Supported in almost all programming languages, it's used in many areas such as data validation, text processing, and log analysis.

markdown-guide.title: Markdown Guide: Quick and Easy Documentation
markdown-guide.description: Markdown syntax, use cases and best practices. Ideal format for README, documentation, and blog posts.
markdown-guide.content: Markdown is a lightweight markup language for converting plain text to formatted text. Created by John Gruber in 2004, it's widely used on platforms like GitHub, Stack Overflow, and Reddit.

sql-optimization.title: SQL Optimization: Tips for Faster Queries
sql-optimization.description: SQL query optimization, index usage, best practices. Guide to improving database performance.
sql-optimization.content: SQL optimization involves improvements to make database queries run faster. Database performance can be significantly improved through proper index usage, understanding query plans, and following best practices.

password-security.title: Secure Password Creation: Best Practices Guide
password-security.description: Strong password creation, password management and security best practices. Essential information for account security.
password-security.content: Strong passwords are the foundation of online security. Passwords should contain at least 12 characters, including uppercase and lowercase letters, numbers, and special characters. Password manager usage and two-factor authentication (2FA) are recommended.
